h1: 4 Best Practices for Optimizing Injection Molding Tolerances

headings.text: 4 Best Practices for Optimizing Injection Molding Tolerances
2. Select the ideal material for the application

meta_description: Here are some injection molding tolerance guidelines that designers and engineers can follow to optimize part tolerances.

sections.heading: 4 Best Practices for Optimizing Injection Molding Tolerances
sections.text: 4 Best Practices for Optimizing Injection Molding Tolerances Published on December 14, 2020 Originally published on fastradius.com on December 14, 2020 Injection molding is a versatile manufacturing method ideal for mass production. The process involves injecting molten thermoplastics or thermosets into durable metal molds, ejecting the part once it has cooled, and then repeating that process. This allows manufacturers to quickly and economically produce high volumes of identical components that reliably meet strict mechanical requirements. While factors like material shrinkage — which occurs naturally as the plastic sets — can be fairly easily predicted, slight variations between parts are...
